category_title: Children with autism who exhibit disruptive or avoidant behaviors (shouting, random clicking) during difficult reading tasks
minimal_context: The student is taking a standardized reading assessment with a familiar teacher and is asked to read a passage and retell the story.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please read this passage out loud for the assessment.

STUDENT: (Reads slowly) The boy was walking and talking.

TUTOR: Now, please tell me what happened in the story.

STUDENT: (Long pause) Boy was walking and talking.

TUTOR: Can you formulate a different answer to tell me more?

STUDENT: (Shouts) No! (Looks back at the page and reads with errors) Boy has... walking... talking.

options.A: Acknowledge any requests the student may have made for a break to support functional communication.
options.B: Ask the student what they notice about the passage or how they know it is nonfiction.
options.C: Tell the student that their response was a good idea during the reading assessment.
options.D: Respond to the student by saying, "Another one already? No way! Good job, keep going."
